why_this_step: This is the first session for the open-agent-knowledge-base goal. The experience library is empty, and the LLM Wiki has no expert models or patterns yet. Per the rules, Step A (form identification) must run when a user need first enters. We need to identify the output genre, top practitioners, and ground-truth forms before any methodology or product work can proceed.

evidence.claim: Anthropic 'Building Effective Agents' is a top practitioner sample for agent architecture writing; published Dec 19, 2024; defines workflows vs agents and 5 workflow patterns.
LangChain 'What is an AI agent?' by Harrison Chase is a top practitioner sample for agent definition writing; published Jun 28, 2024; introduces agentic spectrum concept.
Anthropic article content was successfully fetched and saved on 2026-04-28.
LangChain article content was successfully fetched and saved on 2026-04-28.

next_step: Step B: Methodology learning. Reverse-engineer the 2-3 top samples (Anthropic, LangChain, and optionally a Chinese deep-dive source) to extract reusable writing methodology: structure templates, argument flow patterns, evidence density standards, and anti-listicle techniques. Archive brain baseline and diff against research findings.
